commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=865c48bace6212c5fd846dd076eaa0a475f99bec)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Control UI assistant avatar localStorage from one global key to agent-scoped keys, threads the active agent id through render/controller paths, and adds controller regression tests.

PR surface: Source +16, Tests +29. Total +45 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main reads and writes all assistant avatar overrides through one localStorage key, so the linked two-agent overwrite path is source-reproducible without a live browser run.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the branch has code findings plus a contributor real-proof gap that automation cannot satisfy.

target.title: fix(ui): scope avatar storage per agent ID
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- The avatar storage used a single global localStorage key "openclaw.control.assistant.v1", causing setting an avatar for one agent to overwrite it for all agents.
- The localStorage key is now scoped per agent ID: "openclaw.control.assistant.v1:{agentId}".
- Backward compatibility: existing global avatars are used as fallback when no agent-specific avatar exists.

Why does this matter now?

- Users with multiple agents (e.g. main, worker) cannot set different avatars for each agent.
- Setting an avatar for one agent silently overwrites the avatar for all other agents.

What is the intended outcome?

- Each agent has its own avatar stored separately in localStorage.
- Clearing one agent's avatar does not affect other agents.
- Agents with no specific avatar fall back to the global key.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to avatar upload, display, or server-side storage.

## Linked context

Closes #90890

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Setting avatar for one agent changes it for all agents.
- Real environment tested: Windows 10, Node.js v22.11.0, OpenClaw latest main (e99ab385cf).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Read ui/src/ui/storage.ts and confirmed localAssistantIdentityKey() generates per-agent keys with fallback to global key.
  - Read ui/src/ui/controllers/assistant-identity.ts and confirmed setAssistantAvatarOverride() passes state.assistantAgentId to saveLocalAssistantIdentity().
  - Ran regression test: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/ui/controllers/assistant-identity.test.ts
- Evidence after fix: Regression test output:
  Tests 4 passed (4)
  scopes avatar storage per agent ID (regression #90890)
  falls back to global key when agent-specific key is not set
- Observed result after fix: Each agent has its own avatar; clearing one does not affect the other; global key is used as fallback.
- What was not tested: Live Control UI test with multiple agents (requires running gateway + browser).
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The regression test uses localStorage mock; it does not prove browser behavior directly.

## Tests and validation

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/ui/controllers/assistant-identity.test.ts — 4/4 passed

## Risk checklist

- userVisible: Yes (avatars now scoped per agent)
- configEnvMigration: No (backward compatible with global key)
- securityAuthNetwork: No
- Mitigation: Backward compatible; existing global avatars used as fallback.

## Current review state

- Pre-existing CI failures (not from this PR): checks-node-core-runtime-infra-process and checks-node-core-runtime-media-ui are flaky tests unrelated to this change.
- Ready for review after this update is pushed.
- [x] AI-assisted (built with Claude Code)
